PREDICTION_UNIT: One row represents one astronomical object. Predict whether the object is GALAXY, STAR, or QSO.
DOMAIN_CONTEXT: Stellar object classification from SDSS-like tabular astronomy data. Useful signal is likely in broadband magnitudes u/g/r/i/z, adjacent colors, redshift, spectral_type, galaxy_population, and possible synthetic generation artifacts in id or sky coordinates. The public auxiliary original_sdss17/star_classification.csv includes alpha, delta, u, g, r, i, z, redshift, class, and additional SDSS columns.
AVAILABLE_COLUMNS: Train columns: id, alpha, delta, u, g, r, i, z, redshift, spectral_type, galaxy_population, class. Test columns: id, alpha, delta, u, g, r, i, z, redshift, spectral_type, galaxy_population. Submission columns: id, class. Target classes are GALAXY, STAR, and QSO. Numeric columns include sky coordinates alpha/delta, magnitudes u/g/r/i/z, redshift, and id. Categorical columns include spectral_type and galaxy_population.
PREVIOUS_SOLUTION_MODES: The current seed is AutoGluon preprocess code from AIDE run 2-hopping-sheep-from-camelot, seeded from source node 57f1f399122b474da92cce640e6c4b91 and local balanced_accuracy 0.9671848318999143. Infer implementation style from the attached seed code and logs rather than relying on labels.
VALIDATION_CONTEXT: Submissions are evaluated by balanced accuracy, higher is better. The current seed uses AutoGluon medium_quality with XGB, LightGBM, CatBoost, balanced class weighting, a holdout validation split, GPU settings, weighted ensemble, and deterministic preprocessing. Local holdout and public leaderboard may diverge, so isolate one change family at a time.
LEAKAGE_CONSTRAINTS: Do not use the competition target class except as the training label. Train/test unsupervised feature engineering is allowed only when it does not use target labels. Auxiliary original_sdss17/star_classification.csv contains external labels; only use them if the experiment explicitly assumes labeled public external data is allowed. Avoid direct target encoding on competition rows unless it is strictly fold-safe.
EXPERIMENT_BUDGET: Each hypothesis must be verifiable as a single first experiment in under 1 hour on a server-class machine with about 200 GB RAM, 16 CPU cores, and one NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU. Prefer compact feature blocks and bounded AutoGluon changes. Do not propose exhaustive searches, very large ensembles, or multi-hour training.
AUTOGLUON_PREPROCESS_EXAMPLE:
```python
def preprocess(df: pd.DataFrame, aux: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    out = df.copy(deep=True)
    if all(c in out.columns for c in ["u", "g", "r", "i", "z"]):
        out["color_u_g"] = out["u"] - out["g"]
        out["color_g_r"] = out["g"] - out["r"]
        out["color_r_i"] = out["r"] - out["i"]
        out["color_i_z"] = out["i"] - out["z"]
        out["color_u_z"] = out["u"] - out["z"]
    if "redshift" in out.columns:
        out["redshift_tanh"] = np.tanh(pd.to_numeric(out["redshift"], errors="coerce").clip(-10, 10))
    return out
```
